title: Offline-first cache so pit displays don't die when Wi-Fi does
summary: Competition venues have flaky Wi-Fi. Refactored Pulse's data layer around a service worker cache with stale-while-revalidate.
body:
The first version of Pulse just fetched from TBA on every tab switch. That bricks the second the venue Wi-Fi drops — which happens constantly once 3,000 phones join the same network.

New strategy:

* Every TBA response is cached in IndexedDB with a TTL per endpoint (match schedule: 30s, team info: 1h).
* Service worker uses stale-while-revalidate: always serve the cached value immediately, kick off a background refresh.
* A tiny "syncing…" chip in the corner tells you when the background refresh is in flight — so teams know what they're looking at is seconds old, not hours old.

Result: the pit display stays useful even with zero bars. Shipping to production tonight.

> "It's better to show slightly-stale truth with a timestamp than a spinner forever."
